setting.name: The Shattered Realms
setting.description: A basic fantasy setting that can be used with any module. Provides world context without constraining adventures.

world_overview.description: A world recovering from a magical cataclysm known as the Sundering. Ancient civilizations have fallen, magic is unstable, and the dead do not always rest. Heroes are needed.
world_overview.current_era: The Age of Reclamation (approximately 200 years after the Sundering)
world_overview.magic_level: Medium - magic exists but is dangerous and unpredictable
world_overview.tone: Survivors rebuilding civilization, ancient mysteries waiting to be discovered

planes.material_plane: The mortal world where most life exists
planes.shadowfell: Realm of death and shadow, bleeds into material world through old tombs
planes.feywild: Realm of chaotic magic and fey creatures, distant since the Sundering
planes.elemental_planes: Primal forces of creation, rarely accessed
planes.astral_plane: Realm of thought and divine power

cosmology.afterlife: Souls journey to the Keeper's realm unless bound by magic or unfinished business

the_sundered_coast.description: Coastal region where the Sundering began. Still magically unstable.
the_sundered_coast.notes: Source of most undead and magical anomalies

the_kingdom_of_aldwyn.description: Largest surviving human kingdom. Trying to restore order.
the_kingdom_of_aldwyn.danger_level: Low in cities, medium in borderlands

the_old_empire.description: Ruins of the pre-Sundering civilization. Dungeons and treasures.
the_old_empire.notes: Rich in loot but deadly. Most modules take place in Old Empire ruins.

the_wildwood.description: Vast forest, home to elves and fey creatures
the_wildwood.notes: Elves are reclusive after the Sundering

elves.notes: Deeply scarred by Sundering, distrustful of outsiders

the_old_empire.description: Advanced magical civilization spanning the continent
the_old_empire.achievements: Great cities, powerful magic, peace and prosperity
the_old_empire.end: The Sundering destroyed it in a single cataclysmic day

the_sundering.description: A magical catastrophe that destroyed civilization
the_sundering.cause: Unknown - theories include: experiment gone wrong, divine punishment, war between archmages
the_sundering.effects: Mass death, collapse of magic, undead plague, geographical devastation

the_dark_years.challenges: Undead hordes, magical instability, starvation, disease
the_dark_years.heroes: Many small heroes kept humanity alive

the_reclamation.description: Rebuilding, reclaiming land, rediscovering lost knowledge
the_reclamation.progress: New kingdoms formed, trade routes restored, population growing
the_reclamation.challenges: Still many ruins unexplored, undead still rise, magic still dangerous

magic.nature: Magic is real but dangerous and unpredictable since the Sundering
magic.practitioners: Rare - most magic users are self-taught or learned from fragments
magic.restrictions: No great magic schools remain. Knowledge is scattered.
magic.effects: Spells can backfire or have unintended consequences
magic.undead: The Sundering weakened the barrier between life and death. Undead rise easily.
magic.artifacts: Many powerful items from Old Empire remain buried in ruins

why_adventure: Reclaim lost treasures from Old Empire ruins
Find and secure magical artifacts before they cause harm
Protect trade routes and settlements from monsters
Earn gold and fame in a dangerous world
common_threats: Cultists trying to repeat the Sundering

gm_guidance.tone: Dark but hopeful - the world is dangerous but worth saving
gm_guidance.player_motivation: Gold, glory, and making the world safer
gm_guidance.moral_ambiguity: Not everything is black and white. Survivors had to make hard choices.
gm_guidance.mystery: What caused the Sundering? Can it happen again? Let players discover clues.
gm_guidance.death: Death is common but meaningful. Dead PCs can inspire future heroes.
gm_guidance.magic_items: Old Empire artifacts are powerful but rare. Finding one is special.
gm_guidance.undead: Treat undead with horror - they're not just XP, they're tragic victims of the Sundering
